title: nepeta lasiocephala
intro: Nepeta lasiocephala, also known as White Leaf Catmint, is a perennial plant species in the mint family that is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Nepeta lasiocephala include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve anxiety and stress, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
